section1.title: What is a corporate web application?
section1.p1: Corporate web applications are systems for internal use within an organization. They address specific needs: accounting, document flow, task management, reporting, and automation of routine work.
section1.p2: Such an application optimizes processes, reduces time spent on manual tasks, and cuts down on errors. It is built around your structure and way of working, not the other way around.

section2.title: Who needs a corporate web application?
section2.lead: A corporate solution is right for you if:
items: Your company has 10+ people and processes outgrow spreadsheets and messengers
You need a unified system for records, requests, tasks, or documents
You want to automate routine operations and reduce human error
Access control is required: different roles, departments, permission levels
You need to consolidate multiple tools into one platform
section2.footer: If at least two points apply - a corporate application will solve your challenge.

section3.title: What do you get?
items: A web application designed around your business processes
Integration with existing services (ERP, CRM, email, messengers)

section4.title: How we build it
items: Analysis of your current structure and processes - we look at how work is organized, find bottlenecks and automation opportunities, then design and build a web application to optimize those processes.
Build to a specific request - if you already have a clear brief or task description, we create a web application to your requirements without a prior analysis phase.
section4.footer: In both cases you get a working application that fits your environment and is ready to evolve.

section7.title: Support and evolution
section7.p1: We don't abandon the project after launch. We offer technical support, iterative improvements as your business grows, and onboarding for new employees.
section7.p2: You can scale the system: add modules, integrations, and users without rebuilding the architecture.

corporateFaq.q: How is a corporate app different from a regular website?
corporateFaq.a: A corporate app is an internal tool for work: task management, documents, employees. A website is a storefront for clients. They are different products with different goals.
On average 4 to 12 weeks, depending on complexity. Simple systems (tasks, requests) are faster. Complex platforms with integrations take longer.

corporateFaq.q: Can it integrate with ERP, CRM, or other systems?
corporateFaq.a: Yes. We connect any service via API: ERP systems, CRM platforms, Telegram, email, and more.

corporateFaq.q: Do I need a dedicated server?
corporateFaq.a: Not necessarily. We can deploy to the cloud (AWS, Google Cloud, Hetzner) or your own servers - it depends on security requirements and budget.

corporateFaq.q: Can the application be extended after launch?
corporateFaq.a: Of course. The architecture is designed for growth: you can add modules, expand functionality, and connect new integrations.
We use encryption, role-based access control, action logging, and regular backups.

corporateFaq.q: Will there be training for employees?
corporateFaq.a: Yes. We provide training, prepare documentation, and help onboard new users.

corporateFaq.q: How much does a corporate application cost?
corporateFaq.a: Cost depends on the scope of features, number of integrations, and business logic complexity. You can get a preliminary estimate using our calculator.

custom.label: Custom development
pros: Full control over code and data

offshelf.label: Off-the-shelf solution (SaaS / boxed)
cons: Monthly fees that grow with scale
